subsection.heading: Authorization.—
subsection.chapeau: Necessary amounts are authorized to be appropriated—

paragraph.chapeau: to enable the Mayor of the District of Columbia to provide additional municipal services in the District of Columbia during the inaugural period, including—

subparagraph.content: employment of personal services without regard to chapters 33 and 51 and subchapter III of chapter 53 of title 5;
travel expenses of enforcement personnel, including sanitarians, from other jurisdictions;
the hiring of the means of transportation;
meals for policemen, firemen, and other municipal employees;

the cost of removing and relocating streetcar loading platforms, construction, rent, maintenance, and expenses incident to the operation of temporary public comfort stations, first-aid stations, and information booths; and
other incidental expenses in the discretion of the Mayor; and

paragraph.content: to enable the Secretary of the Interior to provide meals for the members of the United States Park Police during the inaugural period.
subsection (a)(1) of this section are payable in the same way as other appropriations for the expenses of the District of Columbia; and
subsection (a)(2) of this section are payable in the same way as other appropriations for the expenses of the Department of the Interior.

html:td.html:p: 36:723 (words between 1st and 2d commas and between next-to-last and last commas).

html:td.style: text-align:left; vertical-align:top; border-left:1px solid black; padding-left: 2pt;

note.p: In subsection (a)(1)(A), the words “chapters 33 and 51 and subchapter III of chapter 53 of title 5” are substituted for “the civil-service and classification laws” for clarity and consistency in the revised title and with other titles of the United States Code.

note.heading: Amendments
note.p: 2002—Pub. L. 107–155 renumbered section 510 of this title as this section.

note.heading: Effective Date of 2002 Amendment
note.p: Amendment by Pub. L. 107–155, effective Nov. 6, 2002, see section 402 of Pub. L. 107–155, set out as an Effective Date of 2002 Amendment; Regulations note under section 30101 of Title 52, Voting and Elections.
